zh.lifeSummary: 當William Yelverton IV, Esq於大約1503年出生在英國諾福爾可盧加姆時，他的父親William Yelverton III, Esq25歲，母親Margaret Gamond10歲。他於1526年與Lady Anne Fermour結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和5個女兒的父母。他去世於1586年在他的家鄉享年84歲，埋葬在英國諾福爾可盧加姆。

nameMeanings.name: Yelverton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Devon): habitational name from either of two places called Yelverton, in Norfolk or Devon. Medieval examples of the surname are from the Norfolk place, but the modern surname in Britain is associated mainly with Devon, where the earliest record of the surname is found in 1761, and is probably a substitution for Elford , an earlier form of the Devon placename. The Devon place, a hamlet in Buckland Monachorum, is not officially on record as Yelverton until the 19th century, when the Great Western Railway so named the station it built there in 1859. It is a dialect pronunciation of Elverton, which is first recorded in 1765 as an extended form of its medieval name Elford + -ton ‘town’. The Norfolk placename is recorded as Ailuertuna in 1086, and probably derives from the Old English personal name Æthelfrith or Geldfrith + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’.

variants.name: Elford
meanings: English: habitational name from Yelverton in Buckland Monachorum (Devon), recorded as Elleford in 1291 (see Yelverton ), named from the Old English personal name Ella or elle, ellen ‘elder tree’ + ford ‘ford’. The name may also derive from any of the places called Elford in Staffordshire and Northumberland, both probably from the Old English personal name Ella + ford.

variants.name: Yelvington
meanings: Probably an altered form of English   Yelverton  .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

en.lifeSummary: When William Yelverton IV, Esq was born about 1503, in Rougham, Norfolk, England, his father, William Yelverton III, Esq, was 25 and his mother, Margaret Gamond, was 10. He married Lady Anne Fermour in 1526. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 5 daughters. He died in 1586, in his hometown, at the age of 84, and was buried in Rougham, Norfolk, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William Yelverton IV, Esq est né environ 1503, à Rougham, Norfolk, Angleterre, son père, William Yelverton III, Esq, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Margaret Gamond, avait 10 ans. Il a épousé Lady Anne Fermour en 1526. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 5 filles. Il est décédé en 1586, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 84 ans, et a été enterré à Rougham, Norfolk, Angleterre.

ko.lifeSummary: William Yelverton IV, Esq(이)가 대략 1503년 영국 노르폴크 루그함에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William Yelverton III, Esq(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret Gamond(은)는 10세였다. 그는 1526년 Lady Anne Fermour(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1586년 그의 고향에서 84세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 노르폴크 루그함에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William Yelverton IV, Esq родился около 1503 г. в местности роугам, норфолк, Англия, его отцу по имени William Yelverton III, Esq было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret Gamond, 10. Он женился на женщине по имени Lady Anne Fermour в 1526 г. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 5 дочерей. Он умер в 1586 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 84 лет и был похоронен в местности роугам, норфолк, Англия.
